creator: Clockwork6400@protonmail.com
text:
Created attachment 239830
coredump.txt.2

Hello.

Here's what's going on: A few months ago, XORG stopped working for me on this device. I then had to set the IgnoreABI option in the nvidia settings to fix this problem. It would seem that everything was fine. But recently, after some kind of package update, my desktop environment stopped running - lumina with my i3wm window manager. I had to use only i3wm. After some time the problems started again. My system periodically went into kernel panic!!! And recently, she began to go into kernel panic very often. Several times a day. It became completely impossible to work. I had to disable the video card and use free drivers. And guess what happened? On drm-kmod my DE works great and hasn't gone into kernel-panic yet. It seems to me that the problem lies in the driver or somewhere deeper.

Attached to this bug is an attachment with my core dump.

It is worth noting that I am using an outdated driver and the following packages

nvidia-driver-340-340.108_3
nvidia-xconfig-470.86
nvidia-settings-470.86

for my graphics card on this device:

vgapci0@pci0:1:0:0: class=0x030000 rev=0xa2 hdr=0x00 vendor=0x10de device=0x0a65 subvendor=0x1043 subdevice=0x8334
     vendor = 'NVIDIA Corporation'
     device = 'GT218 [GeForce 210]'
     class=display
     subclass = VGA

So far I'm using drm-kmod, but now I have to make do with one monitor. This is very inconvenient for work.
